title: Verb Conjugation - Present Tense
description: Learn to conjugate regular -ar, -er, and -ir verbs in the present tense and use object pronouns.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'to tolerate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Exactly, tolerar. It's always going to be -ar, -er, or -ir. To say 'I tolerate', you remove the ending and add -o. How would you say 'I tolerate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Remember to move the accent back in the present tense. What was 'to eat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And how do you say 'I eat'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Como. Good. Now, most verbs in Spanish are -ar verbs. For example, tuitear means 'to tweet'. How would you say 'to eliminate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I eliminate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Elimino. Good. For 'he/she/it/you (formal)', just use an 'a' at the end. How would you say 'he eliminates'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Elimina. Good. How would you say 'to last', as in 'it lasts a long time'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Durar. How would you say 'it lasts a long time'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No dura mucho. How do you say 'to exaggerate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'I exaggerate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Exagero. Good. How would you say 'he exaggerates'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Exagera. Good. How would you say 'to chew'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Masticar. How would you say 'I chew'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Mastico. Good. How would you say 'he chews'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Mastica. Good. How would you say 'to collaborate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'I collaborate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Colaboro. Good. How would you say 'he collaborates'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Colabora. Good. How would you say 'to speak'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How do you say 'I speak'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablo. Good. How would you say 'they're not speaking with Pablo'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I want to speak'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'I'm going to speak'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'you speak' informally?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Hablas. Good. How would you say 'I want it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I want to see it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'it's important to speak about it'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. How would you say 'I must invite him'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. That's how we use these little words. Well done.

outro.text: You've learned how to conjugate regular verbs in the present tense and use object pronouns.
Try conjugating some new verbs you know in the present tense and see how they fit into sentences.
With practice, these conjugations will become second nature, allowing you to express yourself more fluently.
